title: brickellia eupatorioides var. eupatorioides
intro: Brickellia eupatorioides var. eupatorioides, also known as False Eupatorium or False Boneset, is a plant species native to North America, particularly in the southwestern United States and Mexico. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties, making it useful in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The health benefits of Brickellia eupatorioides var. eupatorioides include its ability to reduce fever, alleviate pain, and promote the healing of wounds. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tea or tincture by infusing the leaves and stems in hot water or a solvent like ethanol, which is often used to treat various health conditions.
